title: Consistency is the real edge
text: One of the most powerful forces in life is consistency. Being able to do something in repetition over and over and over again. That's how you start big movement. That's how you get big results. That's how you build a body. That's how you build a business is consistency. It's small wins every single day.

title: Bible quote - 2 Thessalonians 3:10-12
text: And that's why the Bible goes after laziness as a sin. Because laziness is essentially saying, God, you have given me nothing of significance and importance. Therefore, I can fritter my life away being lazy, not developing my physical talents or my intellectual talents or my spiritual talents. Because guess what? It really doesn't matter. And God says, no, you matter. I've given you talents. Now, they're dormant, a lot of them. So you've got to develop them. Which means you've got to get up, off your backside cliff, and you've got to start working. You've got to develop your body. You've got to develop your mind. And you've got to develop a relationship with me.

title: Why We Took the Risk of Starting SEVN Together
text: I'm not an advocate for necessarily just partnering up with your parents, your brothers, your sisters, your best friends, because unfortunately the truth of the matter and the stats are there, it might lead to losing friendships. This is something that we thought a lot about going into that venture. Obviously the fact that my partner and I have been working together for the last four years and we work so well together, we knew that it was kind of a no-brainer. My brother actually started his own e-commerce business with his partner about eight years ago. They launched a very successful eight-figure dog subscription box. We all have one thing in common, which is a very big passion for fitness. For us, it was kind of a no-brainer in the sense that we all get along very well. We each kind of already know our roles in this venture. That's the reason why we decided to go ahead with it. Make sure that you truly align on what's your end goal, what's your vision, and seeing like, well, if we don't align on this, how is it going to work? I'm not saying that you guys should both be identical, right? I think that when two people are kind of different in certain things, they complement each other, especially in business. But if you don't necessarily have the same vision and you don't want to go on the same path, unfortunately, it's sad to say, but it might probably hit a wall, you know?

title: Day in the life - Episode 1
text: I would say I work out almost every single day. Not necessarily at the gym, but I try to do a type of exercise every single day. As you guys know, I'm starting a performance supplement brand. The first product that we're going to be releasing is actually an electrolyte stick. I'm excited for you guys to try it out. I just want to be on my own. That's what it shifted for me. When I really started at the bottom in a call center, 150, 200 calls a day, and realizing that it's only up to me if I want to go up from here. We're trying to increase his protein intake, get some muscle on him. Hey, Jordan. Jordan's name tattooed here. I've been wanting tattoos since I'm young, man, but I finally pulled the trigger this last year and got a sleeve tattoo. Obviously, my parents were super happy. Nothing makes a immigrant parent more happy than a nice sleeve tattoo. This is how we let out steam throughout the day. So, I was just actually just on a call right now with a client that we had pitched him our services last year. He got very busy. He's a business owner managing actually a few businesses. I followed up with him, and he's ready to meet. I'm in the process of rebooking the next meeting. We're going to close up shop for today, and it's Friday. So, I'm most excited about going home to my wife and son. And that's it, boys. So, now we're heading out. I'm going to actually walk back home. Appreciate you guys tuning in. Thanks for watching.

title: The story behind the name Jordan
text: How did you decide to call your son Jordan? Actually, that's a great question, man. So a lot of people don't know, but my parents are Jordanian-Romanian, so my dad's from Jordan. My mom's from Romania. I was born in Jordan, came here when I was about 11 months old. We decided to go for the name Jordan. It has a lot of significance for us. As well, you know, we thought about the Jordan River and, you know, it's something that's super close to our faith. So yeah, that's why we went for the name. So it's definitely not for Michael Jordan. It's more for personal reasons. Here, I'll actually show you guys something pretty cool. So my brother, being the godfather of Jordan, got him this. It's a 92 Dream Team jersey of Michael Jordan. So a lot of people don't know, but he actually used to wear number nine when he played with the U.S. Olympic team. And yeah, I just thought it was an amazing gift. I usually take meetings from here, and clients always ask me, he's like, who's that Jordan number nine? You know, they always think it's, he wore 23, but yeah, I actually wore number nine. And I'm excited to hand it out to Jordan one day when he's old enough to understand what it is.

title: Starting @takesevn
text: as you guys know i'm starting a performance supplement brand with some of my good friends the first product that we're going to be releasing is actually an electrolyte stick we're pretty much done so this is actually a lemon lime flavor so good so we're creating a product that will have close to like 500 milligrams of sodium 200 milligrams of potassium and about 70 milligrams of magnesium so we think that's like the perfect sweet spot for someone who's active but doesn't necessarily want to run a marathon every single day but just wants to make sure that he's hydrated properly cognitive function is on point everything from muscle recovery to cramping is just significantly improved i've seen a huge difference when i started actually taking electrolytes started about a few years ago when i was training for my uh half ironman and since then i've never stopped but we were never necessarily able to find a product that fits what we feel comfortable putting in our bodies you know whether it's uh no artificial flavors no added colorants no sugars so we worked back and forth with a manufacturer and finally we're able to create a product that we believe is the cleanest one out there super transparent something that's also very tasty we're probably going to be releasing two flavors at first god willing if everything goes well the product should be going to market in around march to april of 2026

title: I started SEVN to be more intentional
text: This isn't a side project. I'm all in. People keep asking me why I decided to start a performance supplement brand. The truth is I already have a great business and this wasn't about starting something new but I care deeply about performance and health in work and training and in life and I wanted to be more intentional about what I put into my body. So we're starting seven because we wanted something clean, thoughtfully formulated and obviously effective and electrolytes felt like the foundation because performance starts with hydration, focus and consistency. So seven isn't about shortcut. It's about showing up every single day at your best.

title: Fitness podcast interview with gates2fitness
text: he's got 250 million under management he's a business owner he's an iron man he's a content creator he just became a father and as if that wasn't already enough he just launched the supplement brand so how in the world did sammy have the time to get into the shape that he did let's ask him sammy what was your biggest challenge balancing your life with your fitness i would definitely say my biggest challenge was finding the time i truly thought that fitness was just about grinding harder you know it was putting more time in the chin how did we change that what surprised me the most was how simple it could actually be and it showed me that fitness and all honesty especially for me it's not about extremes you know it's not about a super strict diet but it's really about sustainability we ended up doing small adjustments i've done consistently and that's what truly made a massive i would say difference over time what was the impact training together had on your aesthetics as well as your life definitely say i got much leaner i got stronger in the gym but what i truly want to say here jay is that biggest impact that i seen personally was outside of the gym you know i i gained more confidence discipline and even you know just clarity in my career one of the the new ventures that i'm starting with some of my good friends and partners is actually a performance supplement brand so whether it's you know it's in business relationships or just how you carry yourself on a day-to-day it will change you i truly believe that

title: Morning Workout
text: Hey guys, so as you can see, I just started the morning in the kitchen with my little guy. You know, we got some milk ready, then made myself a quick shake before heading downstairs to the condo gym. Nothing crazy for today, just a quick arm session. I did biceps and triceps, a few sets of curls, some rope pushdowns, hammer curls, some dips, and just finished off with some cable work. It's honestly not about spending hours in the gym. It's just about showing up, staying consistent, and setting the tone for the day, both for me and for my son.
